$comment: Project-scoped MCP servers (carry over to Claude Code on the web; checked into version control per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp project-scope). Bearers are REFERENCES to env vars, expanded at connect time - literal values never stored here (secrets-as-references). Three remote servers: (1) Schnapp_Mac - the Mac shell/SQL/files/services bridge (connectors/mac-mcp/server.py, mac-mcp.schnapp.bet/mcp); its _BearerAuthMiddleware authorizes ALL tools once Authorization: Bearer ${MAC_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN} is presented (no per-call token). Enabling it grants this surface full Mac tool access: a deliberate, owner-accepted decision (decisions/0014, refines 0013). (2) Schnapp_Secrets - the off-Mac 1Password resolver (op-mcp on Render). (3) Schnapp_Memory - the off-Mac git-backed memory lane (memory-mcp on Render). The cloud-only pair (2)+(3) is Mac-INDEPENDENT: off-Mac Code/Cowork keep secret resolution + memory R/W when the Mac is asleep. They activate only where their bearer env var is set - in cloud environments the owner sets OP_MCP_BEARER / MEMORY_MCP_BEARER, and allowlists op-mcp.onrender.com / memory-mcp-rtad.onrender.com per docs/environment-and-access.md §1. On the Mac these two stay disconnected BY DESIGN (their bearers are not in the Mac env; the Mac uses the local op CLI + git instead). claude.ai web + iPhone do NOT read this file - they reach op-mcp/memory-mcp through the Cloudflare OAuth portal mcp.schnapp.bet (claude.ai's connector UI is OAuth-only). New project servers require one-time approval on first use (Claude Code prompts).
